findings.message: This file dates PR #148's merge as 2026-06-04, but the bundle README and evaluation.md both place the same merge on 2026-06-02. The evidence set is internally inconsistent about the snapshot boundary.
The bullet says `"tmp/reviewer-runtime"` is defined in four separate locations, but the rationale says five occurrences and the locations list contains five paths. The count in the heading is wrong.

`evaluation.md` says the overall density drop is "fully concentrated in cli and cli-composition", but the table immediately above shows domain density also fell from 0.743 to 0.713 (-4.0%). The statement should say the drop is mostly, not fully, concentrated in those two crates.

`evaluation.md` says the before/after census used the same model `claude-sonnet-4-6`, but the checked-in workflow documents and implements a mixed model setup: Sonnet for scan/load/synthesis and Opus for adversarial verification (`dry-scan.workflow.js` lines 6-7 and 335-346). This makes the methodology claim internally inconsistent; update the fairness-control wording to describe the model mix, or adjust the workflow/provenance if Sonnet-only was intended.

The report generator labels every cross-layer finding as “Cross-layer (knowledge) duplications — the DRY gate's prime targets”, but it only filters on cross-layer locations and includes exact-clone/near-clone/data-dup entries, while the evaluation says the gate targets semantic diff-vs-corpus duplication and structurally misses cross-layer knowledge/data duplication. This makes the generated before/after reports contradict the bundle’s own gate model and misclassify the highlighted section.
